title: A whole-slide foundation model for digital pathology from real-world data
core_conclusion: Prov-GigaPath, a whole-slide pathology foundation model pretrained on 1.3 billion real-world clinical image tiles from 171,189 slides across 28 cancer centres, achieves state-of-the-art performance on 25 out of 26 digital pathology tasks by combining large-scale real-world data with ultra-large-context LongNet modelling that captures the entire gigapixel slide rather than subsampling tiles.
why_it_matters: Breaks the tile-subsampling bottleneck of prior models; demonstrates that real-world clinical data scale decisively outperforms curated benchmarks; enables zero-shot mutation prediction via slide-level vision-language alignment; fully open-weight to accelerate clinical AI research worldwide.
why_nature: Paradigm-level advance from tile-level to whole-slide modelling; largest pathology pretraining effort to date (1.3B tiles); direct clinical significance for cancer diagnostics in low-resource settings; open weights and reproducibility commitment meet Nature data-sharing standards.
added_value_to_my_research: Spatial non-stationarity of clinical training data -> geography-conditioned fine-tuning template for geospatial foundation models
Data representation gaps by cancer centre geography -> 'data-2SFCA' metric for AI equity in health systems
Pathology scaling laws (data volume vs AUROC) -> blueprint for data-efficient geospatial foundation model fine-tuning
WSI-report CLIP contrastive alignment -> reusable multi-modal fusion architecture for geospatial data (GPS + satellite + social media)
ABMIL spatial attention maps -> spatially explicit clinical explanations analogous to SHAP attribution in explainable GeoAI

figures.caption: Overview of Prov-GigaPath architecture: tile serialization, DINOv2 tile-encoder pretraining (b), and LongNet masked-autoencoder slide-encoder pretraining (c).
Gene mutation prediction — AUROC/AUPRC comparisons across pan-cancer 18-biomarker, LUAD 5-gene, and TMB benchmarks; Prov-GigaPath leads on all (n=10 experiments, mean ± s.e.m.).
Cancer subtyping comparison across 9 cancer types (AUROC and balanced accuracy); Prov-GigaPath outperforms all competitors on all nine types.
Vision-language image-report alignment — zero-shot cancer subtyping and mutation prediction; Prov-GigaPath outperforms MI-Zero, BiomedCLIP, and PLIP across all metrics.
